Boxy; heavy intramural sport keeps it from going to fat; body is functional, not for being looked at
wears none — eschewed deliberately, not from lack
shorts in warm weather, pants in cold; no skirts

value.correct_statement: She does not know she is Cassandra; the device is never named, explained, or commented on by any character including herself

facts.value: reads as asexual — AUTHOR-KNOWLEDGE ONLY; never surfaces, never named, never any character's observation; shapes rendering (un-watched body, warmth that wants nothing) but is never content
Plain
brown, deliberate — does not twin Randi's black-on-black, does not flatter
Softball in spring, basketball in winter; joins any rec team given the chance
The un-coded third model; opts out of the appearance economy entirely (a 'fourth fact,' not a judgment on the others)
Functional, indifferent-to-venue uniform: ball cap pulled low, headphones, shorts and yesterday's shirt
A marker the reader tracks — voices unease early, gets dismissed, later vindicated
Wears headphones at volume low enough that she can laugh at things said two tables over
Registers structure, not character — perceives arrangement, cost, wrongness-of-fit, and her friend receding
Reports perceptions as flat observations or dry jokes, never as articulated accusations
Pre-nursing student

Registers structure/operation rather than character; reads the operation instead of receiving the beam

Standard double dorm room shared with Vee, featuring bunk beds, two desks, and a bathroom shared through a connecting door with the adjacent room
The Cassandra device: sees clearly and is not believed; never named, explained, or commented on by any character including herself; she does not know she is Cassandra
Heavily involved in intramural sport; reliably competing/away during key narrative moments
Right within the available frame; Vee is right on hers; the reader holds both
Sees Randi clearly, because she has no investment in Randi being what she presents as
unmanaged — not ugly, not slovenly, just not managing how she lands
the body is used, not watched; unmanaged

behavioral_states.trigger: Paying close attention to what someone is saying
behavioral_states.response: Goes very still, pulls knees to chest
behavioral_states.observable: Stillness, knees drawn to chest — 'the way she got when she was paying attention'

behavioral_states.trigger: Taking in / working through what she's hearing
behavioral_states.response: Moves around — window, back, bed, up again — settling as the telling deepens
behavioral_states.observable: Motion that tracks the depth of what she's taking in; settles as the telling deepens

behavioral_states.trigger: Presence of Randi / the visible operator and the friendship's cost
behavioral_states.response: Dislikes Randi, distrusts the friendship, smells the cost; voices legitimate conscious suspicion
behavioral_states.observable: Stated dislike and distrust directed at Randi

behavioral_states.response: Randi's warmth-instrument finds nothing to grip; Cassie reads the operation rather than receiving the intended effect
behavioral_states.observable: Sees Randi clearly, immune to the persona Randi projects

behavioral_states.trigger: Concern about Randi / the staging around Pace
behavioral_states.response: Issues conscious warnings shaped around Randi and the staging, not around Pace's character
behavioral_states.observable: Warnings attach to structure/staging (e.g., the great-aunt look / no-attraction line), not to Pace as a person

behavioral_states.response: Reads Randi for operation/mechanism instead of being caught in the appearance beam
behavioral_states.observable: Sees the mechanism while everyone else is inside one of Randi's beams

behavioral_states.trigger: Witnessing register-wrong behavior from Randi (e.g. the diptych 'Goodbye, Vee')
behavioral_states.response: Voices unease/objection live; is dismissed (e.g. Vee's 'It's coffee, weirdo.')
behavioral_states.observable: Chi Latte diptych — the register-wrong 'Goodbye, Vee', dismissed live

behavioral_states.trigger: Statistics project — sees Randi's too-fast recovery
behavioral_states.observable: Says nothing during statistics project despite seeing the too-fast recovery

behavioral_states.trigger: Late in story, after her early read of Randi proves correct
behavioral_states.response: Says 'You saw it and I didn't' — does NOT say 'I told you so'
behavioral_states.observable: Line: 'You saw it and I didn't'
Highlighter goes down the moment Vee enters

behavioral_states.trigger: Proximity to Pace / Pace's anomalous behavior
behavioral_states.response: Produces true-sideways observations surfacing only as gags or anomaly reports, never conscious accusation
behavioral_states.observable: The state-troopers joke (template: structurally the right question 'who is this man?' delivered as a gag); the meet-cute no-attraction line
distinguish_from: conscious read on Pace as a problem

behavioral_states.trigger: Sport season is in session (spring softball / winter basketball) and a game is scheduled
behavioral_states.response: Is away at a game, unavailable to others
behavioral_states.observable: Absent from home/social context during game times

behavioral_states.trigger: Being at the yellow place on Draper or anywhere curated/trendy
behavioral_states.response: She is there for Vee, with no particular feelings about the venue itself
behavioral_states.observable: Present at a curated venue but indifferent to it; orientation is toward Vee
distinguish_from: genuine preference for the curated venue

temporal_states.value: Reconciled — may matter more than the reconciliation with Pace
temporal_states.after_event: The reconciliation with Cassie at the end of the novel

temporal_states.value: "He looked at her the way you'd look at somebody's great-aunt" — the no-attraction / not-a-prospect observation (full personhood, no desire), which Vee privately recognizes as the look men give Cassie.
temporal_states.after_event: Meet-cute line revision applied in the-meet-cute.md, meta-plan-chronology.md, meta-arch-cassie-randi.md

temporal_states.value: Cassie should be re-blocked to floor / lower bunk / desk chair (not 'on the edge of Vee's bed'); Vee's flop re-blocked to suit a top bunk. Canon is bunk beds with Vee on top.
temporal_states.after_event: Fix applied to vee-tells-cassie-dinner.md re-blocking bed staging

properties.function: Carries the managed/used contrast against Randi's Mercedes as an object, never as a stated comparison

properties.observation: Pace looks at Cassie 'the way you'd look at somebody's great-aunt' — full personhood, no desire / not-a-prospect

relationships.relationship: comparison reference (reconciliation with Cassie may matter more than the one with Pace)

properties.note: The no-attraction (great-aunt look) is directed at the staging/Randi, not at Pace's character

properties.nature: No conscious read on Pace as a problem; he sits in her blind spot; only involuntary subconscious perception leaks sideways

properties.constraint: Keep it a plain object — no name, no quirks, no running bit
properties.description: Old rust-spotted SUV with squeaking suspension; kept because it works and hauls equipment; squeak serves as a free entrance cue Vee recognizes

properties.note: Both are part of the three-model triad (Vee=Art Nouveau, Randi=Art Deco, Cassie=opts out); Cassie is the un-coded third against both

properties.rendering: Cassie exists on page mainly as effect-on-Vee

properties.note: Connection between Cassie and Vee is implied; identity of 'the friend' as Cassie is ambiguous in this excerpt
properties.detail: The friend who would have kept Vee safe was at a game; Cassie's competing-elsewhere pattern partly enables the structural harm to Vee

properties.dynamic: neatness contrast is part of the friendship
properties.Vee_housing: partial scholarship covers Vee's housing premium
properties.bunk_arrangement: Vee took the top bunk without being asked

properties.note: Vee privately recognizes the 'great-aunt' look as the look men give Cassie

properties.nature: Conscious suspicion directed at the visible operator; distrusts the friendship and senses its cost
instant, mutual, established as a given without explanation

properties.basis: No investment in Randi being what she presents as; outside appearance economy
properties.effect: Immune to Randi's warmth-instrument; reads the operation

properties.basis: Cassie's brown palette is chosen specifically to not twin Randi's black-on-black silhouette

properties.Cassie_role: stands outside Randi's beams and reads her for operation
properties.staging_rule: permanent and implicit; never stated/staged each shared scene

properties.basis: no investment in Randi being what she presents as
properties.stance: sees Randi clearly, voices unease, later vindicated

properties.mutuality: the only relationship in the novel built on actual mutuality

properties.nature: Cassie perceives her friend receding; Vee privately recognizes the not-a-prospect look Pace gives Randi as the look men give Cassie

properties.note: Reads Vee instantly; gives full attention when Vee arrives; Vee recognizes Cassie's car by its squeak before seeing it

properties.context: When at curated/trendy venues, her presence is motivated by Vee rather than the venue
